raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I was the victim of a phone scam. I first received a text from XXXX XXXX asking if I made an XXXX XXXX purchase in another state at a grocery store. The text said to call XXXX and provided a phone number if I did not make this purchase. I then fell and hit my head before calling back the number, unknowingly sustaining a concussion. I then called the phone number claiming to be XXXX XXXX, and the person who answered indicated that my checking account had been electronically compromised. He then transferred me to the fraud department. He told me that in order to " undo '' these charges we had to complete a series of steps which he walked me through and described in great detail while he was accessing my phone why he needed to this. While he was on the phone with me he insisted it was very important that I stayed on the phone with him and did not make any phone calls so that we did not lose the call. He then told me the last step was to purchase gift cards at XXXX 's in specific denominations so that I could enter them into a XXXX app to get my funds back. I would like a refund on these funds in the amount because although Barclays claims that the " activity is valid '' because the card was in my possession, I was coerced and scammed at a time when I had sustained a XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. This purchase and this behavior is completely out of my character. I have never made similar purchases and an hour into the phone call I realized I was the victim of a scam. I took immediate steps to remedy the situation including cancelling all of my credit cards, my checking account, filing a police report, and having my cell phone cleaned. This situation has seriously harmed my credit and my mental health. I understand Barclays /Mastercard is backed by FDIC and I am seeking a refund for the amount of {$1900.00} from Merchant XXXX XXXX XXXX.
